etymology.description: 金(金) (jīn, "gold") + 字(字) (zì, "character; word; writing") + 塔(塔) (tǎ, "tower; pagoda") = a structure shaped like a character made of gold -> pyramid
etymology.interpretation: 金(金) (jīn, "gold") suggests preciousness and value, hinting at the grandeur and historical reverence for pyramids. 字(字) (zì, "character") conveys the idea of form or shape, analogous to how a pyramid has a distinctive, recognizable form. 塔(塔) (tǎ, "tower") represents a tall, structured form, reflecting the towering shape of a pyramid. Combined, the word captures a sense of an esteemed, shaped structure, which accords with the pyramids' dimensions and esteemed historical and cultural role.

examples.chinese: 在埃及有许多著名的金字塔(在埃及有許多著名的金字塔)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Āijí yǒu xǔduō zhùmíng de jīnzìtǎ.
examples.english: There are many famous pyramids in Egypt.

examples.chinese: 科学家正在研究金字塔的建造方法(科學家正在研究金字塔的建造方法)。
examples.pinyin: Kēxuéjiā zhèngzài yánjiū jīnzìtǎ de jiànzào fāngfǎ.
examples.english: Scientists are studying the construction methods of pyramids.

examples.chinese: 金字塔是古代文明的伟大象征(金字塔是古代文明的偉大象徵)。
examples.pinyin: Jīnzìtǎ shì gǔdài wénmíng de wěidà xiàngzhēng.
examples.english: The pyramid is a great symbol of ancient civilizations.
